page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized server instance within the n1 family. It is equipped with 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores on an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor architecture. The instance features 624.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.5 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or integrated GPU hardware accelerators. The CPU architecture supports AVX and AVX512F instruction sets, alongside hardware-assisted cryptography via AES.

Benchmark data indicates a clear division between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by bottom-quartile results in stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tests. Conversely, multi-core performance is strong, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark extended instructions scoring in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, achieving strong read-only cached speeds of 3917.68 GB/sec and uncached write speeds of 96.22 GB/sec. Redis SET operations also perform in the top tier, reaching 60.17 million operations per second.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the n1-highmem-96 is defined by its high memory density relative to its compute generation. While it lacks modern instruction sets like AMX or AVX-512 VNNI, its large memory capacity and strong multi-core throughput make it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require the latest CPU microarchitectures. Tradeoffs include weak single-threaded execution and the absence of local SSD storage. This instance is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases like Redis, multi-threaded data processing, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-96 is an x86_64 architecture server featuring 96 vCPUs across 48 physical Intel Xeon cores and 624.0 GB of RAM. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results reveal a significant performance split: single-core execution and single-threaded compression tests fall into the bottom 25%, whereas multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth are highly competitive, placing in the top 25%. Specifically, the server achieves a strong memory read-only cached bandwidth of 3917.68 GB/sec and processes 60.17 million Redis SET operations per second. Qualitatively, the server offers high memory density, though it lacks newer CPU instruction sets like AVX-512 VNNI. It is best suited for memory-intensive, highly parallelized workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing pipelines.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-96 is an x86_64 server with 96 vCPUs and 624 GB RAM. It features strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth, making it suitable for in-memory databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-96 features 96 vCPUs and 624 GB RAM, offering strong multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory instance featuring ninety-six vCPUs, optimized for parallelized, memory-bound workloads and large-scale caching systems.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 624.0 GB of RAM on Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory read/write bandwidth
Excellent throughput for in-memory databases, processing 60.17 million Redis SET operations per second
Weak single-core and single-threaded execution performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
